reviews.title: Super product. Great price.
reviews.text: Really responsive and not overly sensitive. Has been a great addition for tablet. Seamless connection didn’t loose once. Easy to navigate instructions for set up. Great battery life. Used for 7 hours consecutive without sign of needing charged.

reviews.title: Move along. Don't waste your time and money here.
reviews.text:
UPDATE: Two months! Two months this mouse lasted, and that was with very light use. Fully charged, I was just scrolling with it when I felt the wheel break. It just stopped making the little clicky sound/feeling. No trauma, no misuse, just very very very cheaply made. I surrender. It's time to cough up the money and buy the Apple brand mouse for my MacBook. My sanity is at least worth the $60.

ORIGINAL REVIEW:
After three previous tries, I finally got a Bluetooth mouse for my MacBook. I'm hoping this one is sturdier than the last I purchased that barely lasted six months. That remains to be seen, but given the product materials, I'm not hopeful. Making the connection after power-up or sleep mode is a little finicky. Also, a solid flat dark surface/mouse pad seems to be a prerequisite. Without it, the cursor is slow to respond or just does its own thing. I've never seen a mouse with the jitters before, but this one will cause the cursor to jiggle and twitch, even though the mouse itself it perfectly still, untouched. I've watched the cursor jitter clear off the screen. However, using a solid black mouse pad seems to have dealt with the problem. I would send it back and try a different product, but I've sent so many cheaply made and/or mislabeled mice back to Amazon in the past month, I've got return fatigue. We'll give this one a chance with the mousepad solution.

reviews.title: Won't hold a charge - must keep plugged in
reviews.text:
I've been through two of these. I returned/exchanged this first hoping it was just a defect. This mouse will only function appropriately for about 4-5 minutes tops if not directly plugged in to USB , otherwise it starts jumping/skipping/lagging etc. Plugged in it's perfect though - so if you get lucky and get one that holds a charge you'll probably be very happy with it. I just decided to keep this one and keep it plugged in. It's not ideal but it gets the job done.
I should probably mention that it is very easy to set up, basically plug and play - which was nice.

reviews.title: Love it!
reviews.text: Great size and portability.  Saves a slot for other use.

reviews.title: Worth it!
reviews.text: This purchase is worth it. The mouse is slim and connects perfectly with the laptop. Careful you don't lose the mouse in your papers!

reviews.title: Bad product
reviews.text: It worked for a bit and then it just wouldn’t move ni matter if I charged it. Not happy

reviews.title: Lightweight and smooth.
reviews.text: Clicks easily and slides on mouse pad smoothly. A bit frustrating that it goes into sleep mode after a short time when I’m busy elsewhere but ease of use makes it okay. You need to be careful putting in the charger since the mouse is lightweight. And I love the pink color!

reviews.title: I like it
reviews.text: I like most of things to be wireless, and I really like this mouse because I have yet to have an issue with the bluetooth connection. I will say that you do have to push a bit harder to click the mouse than I am normally used to, but it's negligible and ultimately didn't bother me. I like the lights on it, and the side of the mouse works great for me (although it's a bit small for someone with big hands). I would buy again.

reviews.title: Goes to sleep when you need it the most
reviews.text: Won't stay connected to my laptop and goes to sleep.  Have to power cycle to get it to work again.  Even tried leaving the USB cable plugged in and it still goes to sleep.

reviews.title: Looks expensive but feels cheap
reviews.text:
The included USB charging line does not fit snug. Feels like an aftermarket adapter and felt as if the mouse would break is pushed all the way in or pulled out. Time will tell if the charging line lasts. I'll update as time goes on.

It has the feel of a toy but the girls in the house are already asking for one like it, so pretty sells. Color is towards the green scale but works well with seafoam colors.

reviews.title: Cheap and useless.
reviews.text: This mouse feels so cheap, it wasn’t even worth giving a review but I wanted to warn others to not get this mouse. It’s so flimsy and made so cheaply, I didn’t waste anymore time with it so I am returning.

reviews.title: Flawless Bluetooth connectivity with Mac
reviews.text: My Logitech M310 (been using them for 10-15 years) could not connect properly with my USB-C/Thunderbolt MacBookPro, using their USB dongle, thru a USB adapter. Scroll wheel would NOT work, and Logitech tech support said they would not update drivers. 'Don't use an adapter.' But they aren't aware that new Macs don't have old-school USB ports? Anyone else have this problem?! Anyway... this Seenda connects directly with Bluetooth, and connects instantly. If it sleeps (after a few hours?) it wakes and re-connects with one click, in less than a second. I charged the battery when I bought it a few weeks ago, and forgot that I might need to charge it again one day. Still going. Downsides: it is much smaller and lighter than I am used to (the M310 is easily twice as thick/tall) so I am getting used to that. Took awhile to find the right DPI/resolution/speed (and the button to change that is in a precarious, easy-to-hit-by-mistake position. Overall a great recommend.

reviews.title: squeaks when scrolling - not for small hands
reviews.text: Loud clicks, SQUEAKS when scrolling, made for larger hands. You would think since it's pink it wouldn't be designed for a mans hands but here we are. Ultimately, the obnoxious squeaking while scrolling is why I am returning it.

reviews.title: Amazing addition to the Logitech G Pro Wireless!
reviews.text: Great quality, precision cut pieces that fit EXACTLY in the shape of the mouse. Whoever designed the CAD for this did a great job on the measurements. Using the grip itself, also crazy how something so small makes a big difference. No more worrying about it slipping off my right thumb which I mentally couldn't handle. It's shame that Logitech didn't design the grip but this will do nicely until they do.

reviews.title: They're ok.
reviews.text:
I'm only using the side grips. (No issue with the texture of the buttons, so imo, its best to just not use them and keep the mouse as light as possible)

Its definitely an improvement over the stock plastic feel, which is too smooth for me. These add some grip, and even a bit of cushion.

Guess I was expecting a more grippy feeling. Maybe a more pronounced texture. There is definitely room for improvement for those of us with sweaty gaming hands.

I also wish the edges were tapered off at its edges rather than a hard cut, but that's a minor nitpick and not too important.

I will say with these grips, I am having trouble deciding between which mouse to use primarily... G Pro Wireless or Viper Ultimate. This is definitely something that was missing from the G Pro Wireless's design for me.

reviews.title: Love it!
reviews.text: Was expecting more of a Dbrand style sticker, but this is even better. It's super rubbery and grippy. Don't ever plan on missing a shot when you play with this!

reviews.title: It works for G305
reviews.text: I recently purchased Logitech G305, But it felt slippery. Though this product was intended for GPW, it worked well for the G305.

Easily connect to your PC via hyper-fast, sub-1ms Slipstream Corsair Wireless Technology Bluetooth or USB wired connection, with up to 50 hours of battery life. Powerful Corsair iCUE software allows you to precisely tune sensitivity in 1 DPI steps, customize the ten programmable buttons with macros or remaps, and synchronize your mouse’s three-zone RGB lighting with all your iCUE-compatible Corsair devices. With the freedom of wireless and the customizable precision you need to take your gameplay higher, take victory in hand with the Corsair Ironclaw RGB Wireless.

reviews.title: Excellent design and clean software- dollar store plastic mouse
reviews.text:
Worked great for three months; varying software issues and the mouse spontaneous resetting had us replace the mouse with the same one, as the setup and software for this mouse is very easy. The programmable buttons are easy to reach and press. However, if I am paying 200 for a mouse, I expected more than 3 months of use.

Edited to add:
New mouse worked almost two months before the mouse wheel stopped functioning. Went to a different brand. Cannot in good conscience recommend.

reviews.title: Mouse Hasn't Worked Properly Since Day 1, Customer Service Slow to Respond/Ineffective
reviews.text:
I bought this mouse brand new. It has NEVER worked as advertised, and Corsair's customer support team has done nothing to make it right. The mouse couldn't connect via GHz on my Macbook (despite Corsair and its reps claiming it should work on macOS. See:

https://www.amazon.com/ask/questions/Tx1JD042VHLLA6L/ref=ask_dp_dpmw_al_hza

I then tried to use a Windows laptop to connect to iCUE and program the mouse. AGAIN, nothing. They've implied that the mouse might have some compatibility issues, but that should've been addressed! They haven't even offered to refund me or replace the mouse. Stay far away from this mouse, and if you value good, responsive customer service, stay away from this company.

reviews.title: Good mouse for the most part
reviews.text: I bought mine at Best Buy and not Amazon. It lasted about a year and a half. Still mostly works but the wheel button doesn't work anymore.  If you are going to use the wheel button a lot, don't buy this mouse. Other problems include the battery doesn't last too long, can't customize when it is in Bluetooth mode, and it has the habit of thinking every time you plug it into the computer to charge it is a new mouse so it creates a new profile. It looks great and feels great though and I would buy it again if it didn't cost so much money

reviews.title: Loud click, nice feel on your fingers, good for small hands
reviews.text:
Pros:
- Material on your hands feels silky. Great feel.
- Scroll wheel works nicely.
- Tracking is fine.
- Battery rather than recharge, which I like because all of the recharging ones die within a year
- On/Off switch to save your battery
- Excellent for smaller hands. I wear women's gloves size S, and this fits my hands really well.

Cons:
- The click. I have a thing with clicks. It's maddening to hear yourself click loudly all day. If the click were silent, this would be the perfect mouse. Maybe I'm a little neurotic, but it's distracting for me.

Neutral:
- The LED light near your thumb is small. I don't know why so many of mice have light strips. It seems like something else to use up the battery. I'd prefer to not have any lights, outside of what's necessary underneath for a track light. Not a big deal, and better than most.

Other:
- Made In China
- 6D Optical Mouse
- Takes 2 AAA Batteries

reviews.title: Great battery life
reviews.text: I'm still not fully decided about how I feel about this mouse. But, I can talk about what I know so far. The packaging I got the mouse and was really nice, really simple, but easy to open. The mouse was easy to set up, and the RGB looks pretty cool. There is a really cool DPI changer function that makes gaming much easier, however, I can't recommend this mouse for gaming because the ergonomics are pretty trash. I have to cramp my hand like a claw over the top of the mouse and after long periods of use it does start hurting. The surface is also pretty slippery, so I found that my hand slips on the mouse all the time. The tracking however, is great! And I would have to say that biggest plus is the battery life. I got this about 2 weeks ago, charged it once, and it is yet to die! I think the features of this mouse are great, but I would not recommend it. Purely because of the ergonomics, if you are going to use this mouse often, then you probably should go if something more ergonomic.

reviews.text: I like the size and the LED colors.  The battery life is a little lackluster and it actually starts acting up when it's "tired".  I got it for my iPad but haven't used it a month or so b/c I got tired of her attitude.

reviews.text: Will never buy from this company again... the second one we bought froze and would not work again.. same as the first one.

reviews.title: Basic but functions well
reviews.text: I ordered a ‘LED Wireless Mouse, Uiosmuph G12 Slim Rechargeable Wireless Silent Mouse, 2.4G Portable USB Optical Wireless Computer Mice with USB Receiver and Type C Adapter (Silver)’ for review.  For the price point, this is a basic mouse.  The rotating light strip is a cool looking feature.  It is sleek in design.  It connected well with the dongle to my Surface Pro.  It glides well over a mouse pad.  It is rechargeable, which I prefer to battery operated.  The click of this mouse is quiet. The dongle slides into the bottom and of course it has already been lost.  I prefer the dongle to be stored in a closed compartment on a mouse.  It does not require recharging for several days of moderate use and seems to charge quickly.  Overall, for basic use, this mouse works well.

description: 7 Colorful LED breathing light change alternately, soothing light lights up the mouse in dark, adds more fun to the life. Note: It can't be set to one specific color. How to use it? * Charge the mouse for about 2 hours before using it for the first time. 1. Take the dongle out, which is stored at the bottom of the mouse. 2. Insert the dongle into the USB port of your device, and open the mouse switch (at the bottom of the mouse). 3. Allow a few seconds for installing, no action needed, just wait. Then you can use it. Note: If your computer is Type-C port, please insert the USB-C to USB receiver into the Type-c port of the device first, then insert the USB receiver into the USB-C to USB receiver. Turn on the power switch and the mouse will work. Tips: The mouse cursor will move slowly or stop moving when the mouse is low battery. Please charge it on time. The blue light beside the scroll wheel will be on while charging, and then goes off when the mouse is fully charged.

reviews.title: A very nice mouse.
reviews.text:
I tried to use both of the most recent Apple mouse products with my Macbook Pro and never could get the hang of either one of them. I tried configuring them in the applications folder but never could get it right plus neither one of them was comfortable. One had a ball and the other newer one did not. Usually I really like apple products but not this time. I am happy to report this Logitech worked right out of the box after I plugged in the USB thingy. i didn't even have to set it up. I do like the roller ball. I think this mouse can do more than I know about, but am just happy it isn't changing the page I am on constantly. I got the one that was painted blue and green because it was about 6.00 cheaper than the solid colored ones. I like the design now that I have it. Since my laptop only has two USB plug ins, I bought the Amazon USB hub with three additional spots. Am happy with that too.
UPDATE 11-08-14
All of a sudden I am getting these messages saying the battery is critically low. To start with I was changing the batteries every time but it was happening way too fast so I bought a battery checker. Turns out my batteries are fine. Testing 100 %. the mouse continue to operate even though I continue receiving low battery warnings. Don't know how this is going to play out but it is the first sign of trouble. Now I just take battery out. Test it and when it continues to say 100 % I put it back in and the message goes away for a couple of days. Aggravating. I am amending star count down to 4 stars. Can't change stars.

reviews.title: Works great - great little wireless mouse.
reviews.text: I got this for my daughters birthday - she loved the design.  It synced right up and worked immediately.  It worked great with her computer - she really liked he feel of the mouse.  It is easy to pack away and travel with as well.

reviews.title: The middle click and scroll wheel work great! Microsoft 4000 is smoother and more comfortable though.
reviews.text:
I own this mouse as well as the Microsoft Wireless Mobile Mouse 4000 now. I find them to be head to head competitors, so here's a quick comparison.

Logitech:
-Somewhat of a loud click, doesn't bother me personally, but it is obviously louder
-The harder you flick the scroll wheel the more it'll spin. There is resistance though and I feel like it's easier to use than the Microsoft mouse.
-It looks cool and functionality is fine. The middle click is easy to use and the left/right of the wheel to move back or forward in a web browser works and isn't annoyingly used by accident.

Microsoft:
-Quiet click and smoother sliding on the desk
-The wheel stops once your finger leaves it. You can't just flick and go. Scrolling seems more precise with this one, but I actually prefer the Logitech as I feel like I have more functional control with it.
-There is a side button that moves you backward in a web browser, the wheel left and right tilting doesn't seem to do anything.
-More comfortable in my hand.

Bottom line: Microsoft Wireless Mobile Mouse 4000 is smoother and more comfortable, but Logitech is more functional, especially for the middle click and scroll wheel. I was very skeptical about the benefits of this "free-wheel" but I love it now. If you're not a big scroll wheel and middle click enthusiast go with the Microsoft, otherwise the Logitech is for you.

reviews.title: Five Stars
reviews.text: Great.

reviews.title: Excellent Keyboard!
reviews.text: This was a Christmas gift for my daughter and she absolutely loves everything about this keyboard. Thank you

reviews.title: Keyboard is very loud when typing!
reviews.text: The mouse is pretty standard, nothing great, but nothing bad. I brought my cute little set to work in my laptop bag so I figured it was secure. When I took the keyboard out 1 letter had popped off. No biggie, it snapped back on easily. The keyboard buttons are bit higher than I'm used to so I was typing slower and it was a bit uncomfortable after a while. I planned on switching back to my regular keyboard shortly but what escalated that switch was how loud the keyboard was while I was typing. My office is usually rather quiet plus I'm in a cubicle and a coworker asked what are you doing over there!?! He said it sounded like I was hammering the letters with a small hammer. I knew it was loud but after realizing it was bothering my coworkers I immediately put it in my bag to bring home. Haven't used it since!

reviews.title: Nice keyboard and mouse set
reviews.text: This is a great mouse and keyboard set, feels like typing on an old typewriter but more comfortable. I like the combined usb. Looks very nice, comfortable to use,

reviews.title: Amusing
reviews.text: I really like this set. They both match perfectly. The keyboard is comfortable and all the buttons work perfectly. I like the round buttons they are pretty cool. Connects easy enough. Just wish it was rechargeable.

reviews.title: Plug and Play Wireless Keyboard; Not sure about the mouse
reviews.text: I run Windows 10 on my laptop, and this wireless keyboard was up and running in few minutes after unpackaging.  You will need to provide your own batteries (2 AA – one for the mouse and one for the keyboard).  The keys are not tactile like other “typewriter” keyboard I have, but the keys have enough tension in the loaded spring to give a slight “satisfactory” feel to the keystrokes.  The wireless connection between the receiver and the keyboard/mouse was responsive and experienced no noticeable delay.  I am not 100% sold on the “milk tea” colored keyboard, but it slowly grew on me after a few days of use, although my 8-year young daughter thought it was like a puke color… an interesting observation.  The mouse was not for me.  It was too flat and too small for my hand, and after a few hours of cumbersome use, I switched back to my existing mouse pad.  For less than $49 USD, I think this keyboard brings a fun look to the otherwise a boring office environment.

reviews.title: Cool looking but not the best mouse
reviews.text:
I wanted a cool looking mouse to replace a Microsoft mouse that was starting to act funny, after 5 + years. After using it for an hour now I really wish I would have spent more for functionality rather than aesthetic value.

First of all I had to pull out a sheet of paper to use as a mouse pad because my old Microsoft mouse functioned with any surface that I used it on, performed flawlessly. This new mouse can't even be used on a standard wooden desk without skipping and jumping all over the screen.

I prepared myself mentally for a cheap feeling mouse because in the end you get what you pay for but holy cow this mouse feels like something I would buy at the local dollar store it screams lack of quality.

I am sure I will be buying another mouse to replace this one shortly either from frustration or because it was built at such a low quality standard.
